entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop proposed as a potential energy crop and traditionally bred for grain yield and forage quality.

entities.name: ENERGY CROP
entities.description: A crop grown for the production of biofuels.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain harvested per unit area.

entities.name: FORAGE QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional value of the crop used as animal feed.

entities.name: BIOENERGY PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing energy from biological sources.

entities.name: HEIGHT
entities.description: A bioenergy-related trait of sorghum used for genetic improvement.

entities.name: ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to identify genetic markers associated with specific traits.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: A method of association mapping using a whole association panel, noted as costly and time-consuming.

entities.name: POOL-BASED GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: A variation of genome-wide association mapping using pools of individuals with contrasting phenotypes.

entities.name: SSR
entities.description: Simple sequence repeats used as genetic markers to identify traits.

entities.name: SORGHUM MINI CORE COLLECTION
entities.description: A collection of sorghum accessions developed for research.

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL CROPS RESEARCH INSTITUTE FOR THE SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The organization that developed the sorghum Mini Core collection.

entities.name: CHROMOSOMES 2, 6, AND 9
entities.description: Specific genomic locations where SSR markers associated with height were identified.

entities.name: FTSZ
entities.description: A candidate gene involved in regulating plant height.

entities.name: UGT
entities.description: A candidate gene involved in regulating plant height.

entities.name: GA 2-OXIDASE
entities.description: A candidate gene involved in regulating plant height.

relationships.source: INTERNATIONAL CROPS RESEARCH INSTITUTE FOR THE SEMI-ARID TROPICS
